title: Tachiadenus mechowianus
taxonomy: Tachiadenus mechowianus is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Tachiadenus mechowianus is as follows: it belongs to the genus Tachiadenus and the family Gentianaceae. Within the Gentianaceae family, Tachiadenus mechowianus is part of the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further, it is also classified as a dicotyledonous plant due to its seed structure.

The plant's extracts are believed to possess antibacterial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, making it a potential treatment for infections and diseases. In traditional medicine, Tachiadenus mechowianus is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues.
